changes.evidence: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md line 4 — risk_level: HIGH. Reset plan (docs/RESET_SPRINT_PLAN_2026-04.md lines 107-117) explicitly states 'use full SOP for the backend endpoint only' for Sprint 2B, which maps to HIGH under the full SOP governance model.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md line 6 — change_type: MIXED. Correct: sprint modifies API integration behaviour (SSE removal) and API response contracts (polling path shape). No SSOT/knowledge bus content changes.

File read, 440 lines. Fake SSE at lines 403-429: GET /events; asyncio.sleep(1); emits fabricated 'event: status
data: started' then 'event: status
data: completed' then 'event: close
data: done'; no connection to actual pipeline state. Pipeline runs synchronously at lines 166-175: orchestrator.run() called inline inside POST /start, dto returned before AnalysisStartResponse. Polling path: GET /result at lines 339-360 returns stored result via _raw_result_payload_for_analysis_id. POST /start stores result at line 244 and returns status:'completed' at line 274. Option A (remove SSE) is directly supported.

File read. Sprint 2B Task 2B at lines 107-117: confirms fake SSE location (analysis.py ~403-429), confirms Option A preferred, confirms Option B description. Sprint branch naming fix/integration-stability at line 97; prompt uses fix/integration-stability-backend to denote backend-only sub-sprint — no conflict.

File read lines 1-60. healthiq-core-engine role defined at lines 34-50: allowed scope backend/core/, backend/ssot/, knowledge_bus/. backend/app/routes/ not in primary globs. Escape hatch: 'non-analytical API routes unless specifically escalated' (light PR model by default). Sprint explicitly escalates to full SOP. Role assignment is defensible under the 'specifically escalated' clause.

File read lines 1-50. Globs: backend/core/**, backend/ssot/**, knowledge_bus/**, backend/tests/**. backend/app/routes/ outside globs. Escape hatch at line 26: 'unrelated API or DTO changes unless explicitly required by the approved task'. This sprint explicitly requires backend/app/routes/analysis.py — escape hatch applies.

GET /events defined once at analysis.py:403. GET /result defined once at analysis.py:339. orchestrator.run() at analysis.py:169 — single invocation path. No duplicate SSE or result endpoints detected.

Prompt sections 'Scope', 'In scope', 'Out of scope', 'Design rules' are explicit. In scope: fake SSE removal, polling-compatible backend flow, bounded tests. Out of scope: frontend polling, type generation, reports.ts, orchestrator redesign. Design Rule 3: 'Do not modify analysis logic, pipeline ordering, or narrative generation.'

Existing test file: backend/tests/unit/test_analysis_api.py — covers GET /result 404 case and biomarker inclusion. No existing test covers GET /events. Gap: sprint must add a test confirming /events is removed or returns a non-misleading response. POST /start analytical output tests via golden pipeline tests (not named in prompt) continue to pass per STOP condition 4.

RISK-1: SSE removal does not affect orchestrator, scoring engine, or analytical pipeline — determinism fully preserved. RISK-2: POST /start already stores result and returns status:'completed' synchronously (analysis.py:244, 274) — no pipeline timing risk from SSE removal. RISK-3: GET /result endpoint contract unchanged — result shape not modified by this sprint. No hidden side effects on analytical output.

No backend/core/**, backend/ssot/**, knowledge_bus/** files in sprint scope. Design Rule 3 and STOP condition 4 both forbid touching analytical runtime. orchestrator.run() call at analysis.py:169 is preserved as-is; sprint only affects what happens with the result after line 175.

5 STOP conditions at prompt lines 207-213. STOP 1: async job-system redesign required. STOP 2: insufficient truthful retrieval path (not triggered — /result exists at analysis.py:339). STOP 3: file scope expansion. STOP 4: analytical runtime modification. STOP 5: undocumented frontend assumptions. All conditions are clear, binding, and proportionate.
run_work_package.py, golden_gate_local.py, update_cursor_status.py not referenced. No backend/ssot/ changes. No tooling-leakage risk (.codex/, .vscode/, AGENTS.md modification) identified.
Current branch: main. Required: fix/integration-stability-backend. Branch must be created/checked out before Stage 3 (run_work_package.py start). Cursor responsibility per SOP §0.

advisory_notes: healthiq-core-engine cursor rule globs do not include backend/app/routes/ — escape hatch ('explicitly required by the approved task') applies; role assignment defensible.
No existing test covers GET /events — sprint must add a test confirming SSE removal (new test, not regression).
Option A is directly viable: POST /start already returns synchronously with status:'completed'; /result is the truthful polling path. No architectural impediment to SSE removal.

Frontend follow-on (Sprint 2 frontend, light model) should be told: remove SSE listener; poll GET /api/analysis/result?analysis_id={id} until status='completed' or 'error'; POST /start will return completed immediately on success.
